text: >> Hello? >> Hello. >> Hi, this is Chelsea, and I'm reaching out because we pulled up your Medicare profile, and there's a note here showing your 2026 plan update hasn't been confirmed yet. We just need a quick confirmation from you so your coverage doesn't get interrupted. Just confirm for me. Do you have both Part A and Part B? >> Yes, I do. Y'all call me every day and ask me that. >> Yeah, I understand, but benefits are updated every week. You may now qualify for drug cost caps, dental, vision, and hearing. So, let me connect you with the specialist, okay? >> Okay, miss. >> Perfect. Let me connect you with the Medicare specialist from Wisconsin. >> Thank you for holding on. This is Sam. How are you doing today? >> Well, I'm making it. I talk to you all at once in the end and we go, "I told you all that day I came at the hospital." >> So, do you need any coverage on your Medicare, like your cash back food and all the extra benefits? >> Yeah, but I never got it in a no way. >> Okay, so we will definitely help you today. We will give you your food. We will give you your cash back and all those extra benefits that you qualify for. So, this call is in regard to your inquiry about Medicare. You do have Medicare, Part A, and B both, right? >> Yes, I do. I just have one more. I didn't tell you. >> And how old are you? >> 10 to 9. >> Are you still able to make your own health care decisions or you have a positive attorney? >> No. >> You do it best, Alpry? >> Yeah, I do it, Master. >> And which insurance do you have besides Medicare, like Umana, United Health Care at now? >> I do not have United Health Care. >> You're not in health care, that's good to know. We do represent United Health Care in all the insurance skittier, so we will definitely go ahead and provide you all the extra benefit on your United Health Care insurance, like your cash back food over the counter and all the benefits without any cost, okay? >> Okay. >> I got you. And are you getting a U card from United Health Care that you can use it to buy food and pay bills? >> Yes. >> How much money are you getting on your U card? >> $157. >> $157? >> My gas bills, $150. I don't know what went wrong with my gas bill when I moved over here. I was with Lyft, and the last of me, I've been with Lyft since I've been over here. >> So we can take a look into that. We can take a look to provide you more money on your U card, and we can also take a look to provide you more money on your dad's U card as well, okay? >> Yeah, I only do. >> We represent United Health Care, so we will help you, and we'll help your dad as well. That's not a lot. >> And I only get 69 now over food, so I'm going to pay $750 rent. >> Uh-huh. >> Oh, my goodness, you're not getting nothing, you know? You have to pay rent, you have to buy food, so we will definitely help you today. You're in the right place. What is your zip code out there? >> 33206. >> And are you receiving any military benefit, like tri-care VA? >> No, I did nothing but that. >> Okay, are you receiving any health insurance from your last job or retirement? >> No. >> And you're not living in any nursing home, you're living in your own home, right? >> Yeah, no, I went $130 a month, but rent $150 a year. Yeah, I rent the house. I rent some complaints. >> Okay, you have to pay rent, you have to buy food, you have a lot of bills to pay, you know? And you're only getting $150. That's totally horrible. We will help you to get you more on it. >> I'm going to put the $700 all I get. >> Oh my goodness, that's not enough. We will help you to get you more, okay? Don't worry. So can I have your first and last name to get started? >> My name is Gloria, C-A-R-U-T-A-T-R-A. >> Okay, the first name is Gloria, G-L-O-R-Y. >> G-L-O-R-I-A. Gloria, right? >> Yeah. >> And how do you spell your last name? >> C-A-R-R-U-T-A-T-R-S. >> Okay, I only got the C-A-R-R-R-R. >> I've never get people free. >> No, I don't want my man to change. >> No, you don't, you don't. Like, nothing's changed, okay? You'll get more money on your U card, don't worry. Can you confirm your last name again? How do you spell that? C-A-R-I-A. >> C-A-R-U-T-A-T-R-S. >> U-T-H-E-R-S, right? >> U-T-H-E-R-S. >> Okay. >> C-A-R-R-U-T-H-E-R-S, right? >> C-A-R-R-U-T-A-T-R-S, man. >> Thank you for that. Thank you so much. And last thing, I just need to verify is your date of birth. >> About March 30, 1947. >> 330-1947 got you. >> And if you get a chance to increase your benefit, what is the most important benefit that you would like to get today? You need more money for food over the counter, your utility is what is the most important benefit that you would like to get today? >> I would like to have more money for money, too. >> You need more money for food, you need more money for over-the-counter utilities, and you need all the extra benefit that you can get, right? >> Yeah. >> Okay, so we will definitely help you to give you all the extra benefit, and I'm going to connect your call with the Medicare agent in Wisconsin who represents United Healthcare in all the insurance get-years, and the Medicare agent will help you to give you more food in all the extra benefit without any cost, okay? >> Okay. >> And you told me you do make your own decisions, you don't have a power to pay for you? >> Yes, I do. No, I don't have a power to pay. >> Well done. >> Last thing I just need to confirm, do you have your red, white, and blue Medicare card available to get all those extra benefits? >> No, I don't have it. I don't have it right here. >> Is it nearby that you can grab the card easily or it's not? >> No, why would I have to have that? >> Because I'm going to connect your call to the Medicare agents, and the Medicare agent will ask for your Medicare number to lock your Medicare profile because you're going to make sure that your doctors, your medications, and your insurance remain the same, okay? We do represent United Healthcare, so we are not asking you to change. We are just trying to help you to provide you some extra benefit on your United Healthcare insurance.
